title: cordula superbiens
side_effects: Cordula superbiens can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, dizziness, and vomiting due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Ingesting large amounts can also lead to kidney damage, seizures, and even coma in severe cases. Prolonged exposure to the plant's extracts can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems. Furthermore, improper use of Cordula superbiens can interact with certain medications, exacerbating conditions such as diabetes, high blood pressure, and thyroid disorders.
